entry.title: Re: Expense Report Posting to S/4 from Concur and Error Handling
entry.content:
DO NOT USE CTE_FIN_FBL_SEQUENCE_HANDLER to schedule FIN Posting job. This report is deprecated.

Schedule the Feedback Loop (FIN Posting job), through FIN Posting wizard at CTE_SETUP > Financial Posting > Change import > Automatic Import.

If you want to create your own job, create it directly in SM36/SM37 for program CTE_FND_FBL_SEQUENCE_HANDLER, which is the same one used in standard job creation via FIN Posting wizard in newer versions.

SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Concur solutions SAP ERP integration with SAP Concur solutions

entry.title: Re: how to call a OData Servie Of BTP From Abap Report
entry.content: Hi Sandra

entry.title: Re: how to call a OData Servie Of BTP From Abap Report
entry.content: I am facing some issues with my browser haha, but thank you Sandra! This is the link that I wanted to share https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/consuming-a-business-technology-platform-service-from-an-s-4-hana-system/ba-p/13557416

entry.title: Input control : select and deselect nodes in the hierarchy using Javascript
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have an input control on the Profit Center with hierarchy enabled, and I would like to achieve the following functionality using Javascript:

Based on my drop-down selection, I want to include certain nodes and exclude others from the hierarchy. I can currently include nodes with the following code:

IC_ProfitC.getInputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers(["[Y_PROFITC].[MAIN_HIER].&[0000000080]","[Y_PROFITC].[MAIN_HIER].&[0000000031]"]);

However, I am unsure of how to exclude/unselect one or more nodes from the input control.

Can anyone assist with how to achieve this functionality?

Thanks in advance,

Malya

entry.title: How to reverse a UD to continue with DL process.
entry.content: A UD and PGI was made prior to loading complete. How it can be reversed to continue with the downloading process?

entry.title: Re: Data extension Form for Digitally Signed Billing Docu...
entry.content: Did you find a solution to this? I need to update the invoice with 3 extra fields that are not available in the Data Model FDP_V3_BD_STANDARD_GLO_PT.

entry.title: Re: Exception during start of deployment for deploy type...
entry.content: Thanks for your reply,

entry.title: Re: Exception during start of deployment for deploy type...
entry.content: Thanks for your reply, but i have already created destination in my account where TMS is subscribed. I have also attached the image in my post where it give 200 ok.

entry.title: "Manage Workforce" history is not displayed
entry.content:
In Manage Workforce app, for any given employee recently created, there seems to be an issue within "Show Change Log" option.

At first, there are a couple of existing lines when the area is reached. However, the only fields with value are "Changed by" and "Changed on". Fields such as "Field Name", "Old Value" and "New Value" appear empty.

Can this behavior be explained?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: "Manage Workforce" history is not displayed
entry.content:
In general, the entries that originated the worker are not considered "changes". Only what is done afterwards.

Typical signs of such event are:

  • "Change Type" field showing the value "Insert"
  • Fields "Field Name", "Old Value" and "New Value" are empty.

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content: @Matt_Fraser- I was thinking the same thing. They have already provided the /TB6 wage type (Sec.Act -Prev. year comp) to determine which employees are required to do so.

entry.title: Re: ***-stage.lms.sapsf.com refused to connect error whil...
entry.content: @radhar, can you check if the cookies are enabled at the browser level (Browser Configuration Requirements for SAP SuccessFactors)? If you can't enable them at the browser level, the "Enable SuccessFactors Learning third-party cookie mechanism" configuration (KBA 2253268) can be enabled at the BizX provisioning level by an SAP Partner or SAP Support.

entry.title: Configuring eligibility through "Global Rule Details"
entry.content:
Calling all those who are experienced in rigging up eligibility rules. I'm trying to configure the "Global Rule Details" for our Short Term Incentive template to exclude about 40 individuals even if they meet the other eligibility criteria defined in the Global Rule Details. 

Anyone have suggestions on how to configure the eligiblity through Global Rule Details in order to exclude a certain population from the Short-Term Incentive plan template?

I've tried the following:

Creating a "Parent Or" statement for the following options:

1. Job Information. Employment Details. Job Information. Employment Details. Biographical Information. Person ID is not equal to filled in 8 digit person ID

2. Job Information. Employment Details. Job Information. Location. Location Code is not equal to filled in 4 digit location code associated with this population

3. Job Information. Employment Details. Job Information. Location. Location is not equal to filled in location name for this population

 

I've also tried moving either of three options elsewhere within the eligibility logic in the original "AND" statement and "OR" statements.

Any suggestions?

entry.title: Take Charge: Simplifying SAP Fieldglass Company Configurations
entry.content:
Hey, Configuration Manager! Did you know that you have control of a number of SAP Fieldglass’ company configurations? In the Company Configuration tile, you find a list of the available configs that you can manage. This tile lets you enable, disable, lock, and hide configurations related to the different areas of the SAP Fieldglass system, such as Job Posting, Statement of Work, Invoice, and Integration.

 

To find a configuration in the huge list of available configs, you can quickly jump into the related big area of the setting by using the Company Configuration header:

 

But pay attention to the complexity level guidance for each configuration!

When enabling a high-complexity company configuration, a pop-up appears, explaining that the selected configuration may impact workflows and functionality. You must provide a reason and acknowledge the associated risks. We strongly recommend testing these kinds of configurations in a nonproduction environment before enablement.

For the medium-complexity company configs, you see a hover message which includes recommendation to contact SAP Fieldglass Product Support for further guidance.

There are also some configurations that can only be managed by SAP Fieldglass. The hover message explains that you must contact Product Support to change these settings.

Not sure what a specific company config does?

The View In-App Help may assist you! By selecting its icon, you find information about a list of configurations. And if you need to learn more, there’s a link that redirects you to our Help Portal.

 

Are you ready to take control?

Dive into the Company Configuration tile and start setting up your SAP Fieldglass company today. Your time saved and your work made easier, just a few clicks away!

entry.title: Re: U.S Tax Reporter Year End 2024
entry.content:
Hello Community,

Does anyone know if Federal changes for 2025 are forthcoming anytime soon from BSI?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Transport excel report from sac dev to prd
entry.content:
You may have to transport the object (e.g. Story) along with the data. There will be a check box to include data or exclude data when moving. If you can check that checkbox , it should move along with the data. 

Thanks 

Renuka.

entry.title: Re: Advanced Test Orchestration and Tester assignment using Test Plans in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Can we test the enhancement and customisation in above test plan .

What are the limitations of this functionality?

entry.title: The key to the most important FI-CA configuration piece
entry.content:
The key to the most important FI-CA configuration piece

When a new instance of FI-CA is configured, it is almost universally done by the consultancy company that is engaged in the project, and it is almost certain that anything that was done at the previous clients’ implementation, is duplicated from the previous implementation. The current client, not being familiar with the new system, is unaware that the configuration would most probably not be the most efficient for their end-users and reporting systems.

The keystone configuration for FI-CA is setting up die posting combinations that will determine the general ledger accounts the system will post to when that combination is used. In SAP lingo it is setting up the MAINs and the SUBs, and is configurable from:

FICAIMG -> Basic Functions -> Postings and Documents -> Document -> Maintain Document Assignments

In combination with the Company Code and Account Determination Identification, the Main will determine the underlying Reconciliation Account of the Contract Account that is posted to, while the SUB will determine the general ledger account (either Balance Sheet or Income Statement account).

Having a predetermined naming convention and nomenclature can save the company hours of work and thousands of dollars if designed and implemented correctly.

The following guidelines can be used to assist in achieving this:

(1) Never create MAINS or SUBs with leading zeros

This sounds as if 999 combinations are specifically omitted from creation and reducing the number of combinations available to the business. However, since both parameters are alpha-numeric, it would be almost impossible to exhaust the number of combinations.

Main/Sub combinations are used in several reports that are either exported or imported from and to Excel, text or csv files. Whenever these files are opened in Excel, the leading zeros are dropped and when consumed for a data upload will fail SAP’s validation test.

There is also a variance in the number of characters and spaces in the master data table for the MAINs and SUBs (TFKHVO/TFKTVO) and exporting the account assignment data in list format from EK01/EK02. This makes a reconciliation between the two datasets a cumbersome exercise, whereby, if there were no leading zeros present, a simple export of both in combination with an Xlookup in Excel would yield results in minutes.

(2) Develop and use a nomenclature that is geared towards end-users and reporting

The consultancy company assisting with a new implementation will almost universally bring a set of configurations from their previous client with them. Even if this set works well for the previous client, it can be generally agreed that it will not be the best set-up for the new client. Programmers will copy previously used code and implement the code, as is; never taking end-users into account. As examples, a specific debit entry (0300/0230), can be created with an off-set of (0300/0330) and another credit (1500/0840) with an off-set of (1500/0850). In the first case, the debit and credit entries appear to be connected, with the difference of 0100 between them, and in the second case the credit and debit seems connected with a difference of 0010 separating them. In both cases, the debit and the credit end with zeros. This set-up impacts end users negatively in two ways: first when making a posting, and two when pulling data from tables and/or cloud reports.

When making a posting (e.g. with FPE1), SAP will display a list of possible entries for both the MAIN and the SUB when the field is active. Unless the end-user knows the specific posting combo that will be used, they need to scroll through the list and often increase the number of hits that are displayed to find the correct posting. Unless the sign is part of the description, the end-user will not know if it is a debit or a credit entry, unless they display (simulate) the entry.

Noncontiguous or poorly grouped posting combos also make reporting more difficult. An example of a naming convention/nomenclature that can work is to always have SUB credit entries end with 1, 3, 5 and 7 and the corresponding debit entries end with 2, 4, 6 and 8; 9 and 0 can then be reserved for statistical posting combinations.

Cr

Dr

Description

Note

2431

2432

Residential Sec Dep Payment

No requirement to add Dr or Cr in the description as the nomenclature indicates the sign

2433

2434

Commercial Sec Dep Payment

2435

2436

Industrial Sec Dep Payment

2437

2438

Municipal Sec Dep Payment

2439

2430

Sec Dep Request

Statistical Posting

3301

3302

Winter 2024 Rebate

No Statistical Posting Combo required

3303

3304

Spring 2024 Rebate

3305

3306

Summer 2024 Rebate

3307

3308

Fall 2024 Rebate

3311

3312

Winter 2025 Rebate

3313

3314

Spring 2025 Rebate

3315

3316

Summer 2025 Rebate

3317

3318

Fall 2025 Rebate

 

From the examples above, it can be observed that it would be extremely easy to pull data from cloud reports, S4 tables and/or exported Excel spreadsheets, and convert the data into meaningful reports.

(3) Where at all possible, create off-setting entries

When a new instance of SAP is installed, the business will attempt to duplicate settings from the legacy system in the new SAP environment, without knowing that SAP would require different functionalities or offer better capabilities. Certain postings might be considered sign specific, in effect they will always post either as a debit or a credit; an example could be a ‘Promotion Credit”, which should always post as a credit. Since any posting can be reversed with FP08, the initial conclusion would be that no debit posting combination needs to be created for this transaction. However, there are several advantages to creating off-setting entries.

The configuration path where the posting combo’s sign is assigned is:

FICAIMG -> Basic Functions -> Postings and Documents -> Document -> Maintain Document Assignments -> Maintain Transactions for IS-U -> Maintain Transactions for Charges

Define Sub-Transaction -> Configure General Settings -> Maintain Attributes per Company Code and Division

These types of credits are often posted in mass which often requires that some of the transactions also need to be reversed in mass. Since not all transactions need to be reversed, a mass reversal might not be the best.  An offsetting posting combo will allow for selective mass reversals. This will also be the case when individual accounts’ credits should be reduced, either by different percentages or different amounts.

If these reversals and/or adjustments are made in a different period, business often would prefer to run a report containing these reversals and/or adjustments depending on the posting period. This can be accomplished by directly querying the DFKKOP table with the specific posting combo as the input parameters. If the reversals were done with a mass reversal of the original posting combo, it would require pulling the data by clearing document details, which are not always available to the business, and a more complex process.  

Even in cases where the business specifies that a specific transaction will only be posted with one sign, reiterate the scenario where adjustments might be needed. It is also often needed to offset migrated items, and in these cases, migrated reconciliation accounts cannot be posted to, and reversals of such migrated items can lead to ledger imbalances.

Note, through configuration, reversals can be routed automatically to different MAIN/SUB posting combinations other than the original posting combination, or to a generic MAIN/SUB combo that records all reversals. This will still require that all the combinations be created and available for posting.

(4) Avoid Alpha Numeric entries that can lead to confusion.

Do NOT create combinations that include O (alpha) and 0 (numeric), l (alpha) and 1 (numeric) or something similar. This is especially true when only two characters are used; examples are O0 / 0O as a Tax Determination code or 1l / l1 as an interest key code. Especially avoid these combinations if the data field will be used in interface files with 3rd parties.

(5) Create a custom table to hold descriptions for Bill Printing

It is often necessary to have line items on Customer’s bills that make more sense to the customer than the generic description that is necessary for the end user or internal reporting purposes. As an example, we could have different MAIN/SUB posting combinations for several rounds or levels of levies/discounts charged/provided during the year. Below are examples:

 

MAIN

SUB

Internal Description

Bill Print Description

8000

4411

Bronze level credit

Seasonal Discount credit

8000

4413

Silver level 1 credit

Seasonal Discount credit

8000

4415

Silver level 2 credit

Seasonal Discount credit

8000

4417

Gold level 1 credit

Seasonal Discount credit

8000

4421

Gold level 2 credit

Seasonal Discount credit

In this case, the organization can avoid customer inquiries as to the meaning of the different credit levels and why they fall within a specific level. The descriptions on the custom table can also easily be changed if certain posting combinations need to be recycled, without making any changes to internal SAP config tables.

entry.title: Enable/Disable custom action button in SAP Fiori List Report
entry.content:
I have created a Fiori elements list report app. In this app, I have a table with single select radio button and also a custom action button at the toolbar. Now, this custom button will be enabled only when I select any of the row using radio button. (In Manifest.json I have used requireSelection: true). Till now everything works as expected. Now, I want to enable this custom button when user selected any row using radio button + particular column in that row contains a value 'X' (condition based).
How do I achieve that. Please share your suggestions. 

Thanks, 

Teja.

entry.title: Re: Receiving from Production in SAP Extended Warehouse Management - Overview
entry.content: @Jörg_Michaelis  Could you please help me with the link. Looks like it is expired.

entry.title: A New Home Page Awaits: Meet the Layout Manager in SAP Fieldglass
entry.content:
Hey there, SAP Fieldglass admins!

We’ve got some exciting news to share with you! There’s a new kid on the block: the Layout Manager admin object. Think of it as your friendly neighborhood tool that lets you create awesome home page layouts and link them to different user roles. No more generic home pages – it’s all about what your different users need to see to nail their daily tasks!

Why Should You Care?

Imagine walking into your favorite coffee shop. They know exactly what you want, have it ready for you, and you’re in and out in no time. That’s what this new update is aiming for – a personalized experience right from the moment you log into SAP Fieldglass.

This home page revamp was designed to make sure you can navigate easily and see the content that matters most – all in a modern, streamlined design.

No More Customizing Through My Preferences

Yup, we’re shaking things up a bit. You won’t be able to tweak your home page through the Home Page Items in the My Preferences tab anymore. Instead, the new Layout Manager admin object is your go-to.

Personalized Views for Every User Role

You get a set of four default layouts personalized for different roles:

  • Administrator Layout
  • Finance and Reporting Layout
  • Hiring Manager Layout
  • PMO Layout

But feel free to create and configure other layouts the way you want to!

 

Embrace the change and get ready for a more efficient, tailored, and visually appealing SAP Fieldglass experience. No more fumbling around in your home page!

entry.title: Enhancements for SAP Community - December 2024
entry.content:
I am honored to be picking up the mantle of sharing news regarding enhancements (meaning improvements and bug fixes) to the SAP Community. Following are the enhancements released since the last posting.

UI and Bug Fixes

  • Mobile UI

    • Ensured there was a sufficient gap between ''Start a discussion'/'Create a blog' and feed sorting drop down list on feed pages

 

  • Latest Post UI
    • Ensure consistent use of green ticks for accepted solution in Latest Post widget in Question pages Where do we configure SAP FSM Item types? - SAP Community

  • Mobile experience scrolling issue was fixed.
    • Users add difficulty scrolling down the page to get to posts. This issue has been fixed.

 

Community Events Page

Events creators can now select one or multiple industries as a ‘Label’ when creating an Event.

 

Products & Technology Board pages Managed Tags list


The list of the top 10 SAP Managed tags for a specific board page under Products & Technology have been added in the right-hand rail. For example, of the ‘CRM and Customer Experience Boards’ you will find the following top 10 SAP Managed tags:

 

Posting Comment or Answer on Question


It is now easier to differentiate between answering and replying to a question as the distinction between the two has been made clearer with additional verbiage around each option.

 

Floating Indicator


A floating indicator has been added to show which pages have access restrictions in place.

 

Placing of Subscribe button on Posts


It is now easier to subscribe to posts as the subscribe button has now been place at the top of every posts.

Example Question and Discussions:

Example Blog:

 

Custom content block in right column to group landing page


A custom content block is available on Group Landing page to provide additional information or resources.

entry.title: Re: How to get the API Key for S4 HANA Cloud trial (Not for the public sandbox).
entry.content:
I assume you are using a trial version of S/4HANA Cloud Public from https://www.sap.com/products/erp/s4hana/trial.html?

In the FAQ, it says, "The trial system only offers a selected subset of the overall functionality of S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Key user capabilities like ... integration with SAP BTP are not offered in this trial."

entry.title: Re: SAP FICA MAPPING
entry.content: The mapping is done with TCode EK02. Here you can specify the main/sun combination in addition to the company code and division to determine the GL Account.

entry.title: Re: bin determination according to FEFO rule
entry.content: And what about if we have to pick 20 pc and we have expir date in two bins? In my case system determined a bin with oldes stock and stock if stock quatity match. In this case ewm not follow FEFO.

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: BW Series – Getting Started (Part 1)
entry.content:
1. Start/End routine lookups 2. Expert routine code --> same as SQL script in TRFN. You just use Views (stacked if necessary) 

3. Customer Exit variables --> this is indeed missing in DSP. Can be done in SAC with scripting however 

4. Formula variables --> can be done in the analytic model 

5. Hierarchy variables --> same as exit variables, can be done in SAC with scripting or you use hierarchy with directory

6. Virtual characteristics & key figures --> I am not missing these. Caused me headaches anyway. I know for some LO cubes necessary, but I always persisted in BW 

7. aDSO (Type DSO) 8. aDSO (Type Cube) --> views. dimension or FACT. can use CDC to create delta. Can use views with transformation flows to create deletion flags for change log simulation 

9. Multiprovider (Union)/Composite Provider(Union) --> views with union. standard SQL.

entry.title: Re: Connecting SAP Data Warehouse Cloud OData API with PowerBI via a Blank Query
entry.content:
HI Gustavo,

Thank you for the detailed blog

I have performed all the steps successfully, except the last step to obtain data.
When I invoke the function I get the following error:

 

Please, I need your help.

Best regards,
Yonny Serrano

entry.title: Dimension with 200K+ members
entry.content:
We are finalizing on a design for financial forecasting model in SAC and as part of that we have a dimension that has 200K+ members and that dimension is also used to control data access. 

This is basically our central organization dimension where we map all SAP WBSs, Cost Centers, SFDC Deals etc to our Account/Org structure. It will be used pretty extensively for filtering, reporting etc so want to make sure that SAC can handle such large volumes. 

Is having a large dimension like this a problem in terms of performance?

entry.title: Re: Selling Energy Using the Sales Order Integration into Utilities Sales Contracts
entry.content: Great blog @jutta_weissing-hess - Thanks for sharing

entry.title: Re: error DraftsService_SaveDraftToDocument Field cannot...
entry.content:
Estimado,

 

La estructura debe ser algo como esto:

 

{
    "Document": {
        "DocDueDate": "2017-01-28",
        "DocEntry": "3"
    }
}

 

Saludos,

entry.title: HCM Brasil – DIRF - Ano Base 2024
entry.content:
Olá! 

Bem-vindos ao blog post com as atualizações sobre a DIRF.  
 

A Receita Federal do Brasil divulgou em 14 de novembro de 2024 uma atualização referente à Declaração de Imposto de Renda Retido na Fonte (DIRF) para o ano-base de 2024.  

Anualmente, as empresas necessitam compilar e enviar o arquivo da DIRF às autoridades fiscais. Este arquivo detalha o retorno sobre o imposto de renda que foi retido dos funcionários ao longo do ano. As recentes atualizações introduzidas para o ano-base de 2024 incluem apenas uma mudança técnica: a alteração do Identificador de estrutura do leiaute da DIRF para R6GP3ZA. Como o principal objetivo do leiaute S-1.3 do eSocial é substituir a DIRF, entendemos que essa será a última alteração envolvendo esse relatório legal.  

Entregamos essa mudança na seguinte nota: 3545421 - LC BR - DIRF base year 2024 

As mudanças anunciadas são programadas para entrar em vigência a partir de 01 de janeiro de 2024. 

Para uma análise mais detalhada e para acessar a legislação aplicável, recomendamos consultar o Ato Declaratório Nº 35/2024 publicado pela Receita Federal. 

 

Lembramos que as alterações legais que impactam a localização de folha de pagamento para Brasil sempre são disponibilizadas no Regulatory Change Manager. 

 

Gostou desse post? Dê um Like e compartilhe o conteúdo com seus colegas. 

Fiquem à vontade para deixar um feedback, comentário ou pergunta sobre este Blog Post no espaço abaixo. E não esqueçam de seguir a tag  HCM Payroll Brazil na SAP Community para ficarem ligados nas últimas notícias. 

Um abraço, 
Ana Daudt

entry.title: Release Announcement: From Onboarding to Governance: Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud for B2B
entry.content:
On behalf of the SAP CRM & CX Product Learning CoE team, I am thrilled to announce the official release of our new immediate learning journey, From Onboarding to Governance: Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud for B2B.

This course is designed to teach you how to effectively utilize the B2B Organization Management feature within CIAM (Customer Identity and Access Management). By the conclusion of this course, you will be proficient in managing organizations, delegating administrative tasks, and configuring policy-based access control using the CIAM platform.

You will learn how to activate B2B Organization Management and use it to handle member attributes, set up organization approval workflows, manage member profiles, and control their privileges in the user interface. Additionally, the course will guide you on how to onboard organizations using the registration request Web UI, delegate administration roles within an organization, and integrate the B2B capabilities of SAP Content Data Cloud with an external website.

 

So, what are you waiting for? Go to SAP Learning site, and enroll in From Onboarding to Governance: Mastering SAP Customer Data Cloud for B2B. Upon completion be sure to claim your digital badge to proudly display on your professional social network profile!

 


If you have any feedback or suggestions, please feel free to add a comment.

entry.title: Re: working with Multiple salary templates
entry.content:
Hi @deepti_rane 

For Compensation you've got the big 2 items covered already. 

In the route map item we could add "or method of planning" (line manager vs 2nd manager) but I can't think of anything else.

 

In Variable Pay there are more things such as how is the basis calculated (some with 12/31 data vs proration for others, or some with one calculation and others with another for the basis field). On this topic you can see this blog for a design I made a few years ago to go from 7-8 different salary and variable pay template for a customer to only one template (they wanted to improve the planner experience at all costs even if it added additional admin work).

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: Re: EC Time tipps and hints Nr. 7: Negative time vs. positive time
entry.content:
Hi Volker,

Can't time recorders with the Overtime method use a clock in clock out groups  in TT?

entry.title: Re: Configuring eligibility through "Global Rule Details"
entry.content:
Hi @tdiehl12 

I think it's not working because you are assuming that Global Eligibility Rule is an exclusion rule but it is not. Just like the main record rule at the bottom it is an inclusion rule. Understanding how these two rules work together isn't easy so I provided this visual explanation to someone not long ago.

Best way to build this is to build a Variable on USERID. List all USERID in the Variable then add the condition IS NOT EQUAL TO = Yes

If you are not familiar with Variables, see this blog for examples.

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: SAP Access Control and MDG provision date
entry.content:
Dear Expert team, 

 
We have a client transitioning their SAP HEC contract to RISE and adding SAP MDG and Access Control as new components. The provisioning and contract migration are scheduled for March 9, 2025, but the client wishes to start the SAP MDG  project in January 2025. Could the SAP HEC/RISE team provision that component in the DEV/QAS environments as early as January, even though the official subscription and provisioning start date is in March? 
 
Thanks

entry.title: Re: In personas 3.0 how do we wrap a text to show in muli...
entry.content: It depends on what your exact scenario is and what you want to achieve. Is the field display only? If yes, that makes things easier. In this case, you could hide the original field, replace it with a multi-line text control and copy the original field content via an onLoad script into your new field. If the original field is also ready for input, that complicates things, especially if there is any validation tied to the input value. Then, you'd need to take care of taking the input from your multi-line text field, copy it to the standard field and handle eventual error messages as well. I cannot give you one script that handles these different situations.

entry.title: CPI Message Mapping source and target message
entry.content:
Hello Experts, 

I am working on several iFlows for integrations between SAP Products (S4 MDM, Sales Cloud, CPQ) and a 3rd Party. I need to create message mappings. Right now I have got only simple tables with the names of the fields from the source and target system and how they should be mapped. Is my understanding right that: 
In order to do the message mapping I need source and target messages (XSD, XML, WSDL, Swagger/OpenAPI Spec JSON file) which have to be given to me by the sender and receiver party.

  1. Creating an XSD or OpenAPI Spec by myself my on the basis of the tables I got or even sample payloads might be tricky because it doesn't guarantee that the schemes I will get from it will be 100 % corresponding with what the sender/reciver will eventually send/expect (the developement at sender/receiver side is not finalized yet); the recation might be quite complicated as well.
  2. Would using another artifact instead of Message Mapping like e.g. XSLT Mapping be an option? I have no experience with XSLT so I am not sure if it offers the capabilieties required for the mapping (the mappings itself aren't going to be complicated: 1:1 field mapping, excluding fields or merging).
  3. The iFlows are to/from SAP Products, but we will be using some custom fields, so I can't use the APIs available in the SAP Business Accelerator Hub since those are provided only for standard solutions and data objects?

What are your thoughts on this issue?

Thanks in advance,

TG

entry.title: Re: VL150 and VL367 error while creating return delivery
entry.content: S/4HANA: I have the same Problem with some normal sales orders(not all). The products are available but I am not able to issue the outbound delivery. It´s really interesting, that a few hours later, if I try again, I can create the outbounf delivery. I didn´t change anythig, just that some time has passed. Has anyone any ideas?

entry.title: Re: CPI Message Mapping source and target message

entry.title: Re: In SAC, using the Data Export API, how do I export th...

entry.title: Re: Delta processing inside SAP Datasphere
entry.content: Nice blog and interesting approach with the hashing. Will definitely check it out!

entry.title: Re: SAP Adobe Forms - Header Information Not carrying ove...
entry.content:
Solved with a loop to force through the pages.

The problem was trying to find how the mst2 pages are stored and turns out page two is mst[0], 3 = mst[1] and so on. Loop through the pages and boppity boo, tip top magoo!!!!

I created another hidden global field and added the code

Here is the code snippet for anyone that needs it

var pageCount = xfa.layout.pageCount()
if (pageCount > 1) then
    var pageIndex
    for pageIndex = 0 upto (pageCount - 2) do
        xfa.resolveNode(Concat("#pageSet[0].mst2[",pageIndex, "].SubsequentPage.frmHeader.<yourField>")) = <yourValue>
        xfa.resolveNode(Concat("#pageSet[0].mst2[",pageIndex, "].SubsequentPage.frmHeader.<yourField>")) = <yourValue>
        xfa.resolveNode(Concat("#pageSet[0].mst2[",pageIndex, "].SubsequentPage.frmHeader.<yourField>")) = <yourValue>
endfor
endif

entry.title: Reports in Enable Now are missing after Release 2411.
entry.content: Hello - I've noticed that the reports are gone in Enable Now after Release 2411. Were they moved or is there a new way of reporting now? My client uses the user reports.

entry.title: Re: Reports in Enable Now are missing after Release 2411.
entry.content:
Hey Lauren,

Hope you are well.  They have been hidden unless your user account has the User Permission Views: Reports with Personal Data.  Add that Permission to a User Role, such as Report Viewers, and then make sure that you are assigned that Role and the reports will reappear.

This change occurred so that customers that are impacted by GDPR could access some reports but not reports containing the personal information of individual users.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: Re: Reports in Enable Now are missing after Release 2411.
entry.content: Hey Shane! I am and likewise! PERFECT! Just added it back! Thank you!

entry.title: Re: Table refresh after bound RAP action execution on multiple items is not working
entry.content: Thanks Eddy for your Quick Answer. I will check this and update.

entry.title: S/4 HANA License check for specified products
entry.content:
Hi Experts

SAP S/4 HANA provides for a basic framework to check for legal control and license checks. A functionality is provided to indicate if the license type is checked based on Products or is based on a control class.

We have a requirement where only specific products are subject to a license check. All other products do not require a license check should not be subject to a license check in the sales order.

If we define a license type which is based on products / control class, the items that are relevant for a license check would be managed via the license type relevant for these parameters.

However, since all other products are not subject to a license requirement, there is a need that such materials are not subject to a license requirement for the legal control activated for the country. 

How do we achieve this exclusion so that products that are not required for a license check are not expected to be maintained in a different license type ? Maintaining a wider list of products that are not subject to a license check would be a huge maintenance issue.

Would appreciate inputs from experts in the forum. 

Regards

Rohit Zalani

entry.title: Re: Error when deploying Fiori app from VS code -Unexpect...
entry.content: I changed the logging level and it asked me to activate /UI5/ABAP_REPOSITORY_SRV service. I did that but still I get the same error.

entry.title: Re: Inquiry About Time Tracking for Inspections in SAP S/...
entry.content:
Hello AtanasMilev.  In the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, quality management features can be used to manage inspection processes, You can perform quality inspection activities in manufacturing, in procurement, in stock handling processes, and in sales. The request to perform a quality inspection is represented by an inspection lot. An inspection lot can be created manually, or creation can be triggered automatically during the different logistical processes. 

  1. You can create the BTP based built-in functionality  for capturing the duration or time spent on quality inspections . You can learn more with step by step documentation on BTP App creation. This will help you Integrate with time-tracking solutions by developing extensions using SAP Business Technology Platform to capture and analyze time data linked to quality inspections.   You can also create an BTP Application integrated with  SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, set up your development project and perform basic database modeling using SAP Cloud Application Programming Model (CAP) Core Data Services. Learn more here
  2. Record Inspection Results : You can record results for characteristics for one or more inspection lots effectively. For example, you can choose multiple inspection lots at one time and record the results centrally
    While recording inspection results, inspectors can also record the time taken for each inspection operation within the app.
  3. You can leverage Custom Fields App and Custom Logic  Fiori Application that can be used to have the custom fields and logic  in SAP S/4HANA Cloud to add specific fields for time recording to the inspection processes. Here you can use the Custom Fields app to create your own fields and the Custom Logic app to create your own implementations to customize as per your need. Learn more with documentation

Hope this helps

Thanks

entry.title: ATP Check with Batch Determination
entry.content:
Hi All

SAP S/4 HANA native ATP ( Advance ATP is not in scope ) determines the ATP check based on the actual availability of open stocks and expected future receipts. However, the issue is when a customer has specific dating requirements.

SAP S/4 HANA does not allow for ATP check in sales order across batches. Hence, in the sales order, if batch is ignored. the system may confirm the sales orders based on stocks across all the batches. However, since the delivery should only see the items that relevant based on batch determination condition records, we now have a situation where a sales order would be confirmed ( with a confirmation to the customer ), but the delivery would be partially proceed in the system based on allowed batches.

This leads to a customer dissatisfaction. Would appreciate inputs from fellow members on how this can be managed. 

Regards

Rohit Zalani

entry.title: Other-Details tab on PM form not showing milestone fields
entry.content:
Hello, I have a goal plan with a milestone table. using Latest Goal Management. PM form Other Details tab is blank

View of goal:

Goal Plan XML has  milestones table:

<field-definition id="milestones" type="table" required="false" detail="false" viewdefault="off" showlabel="true" field-show-coaching-advisor="false" cascade-update="push-down">

    <field-label>Check-In Recap:  The Check-In Recap meant to summarize the conversation between the Leader and Team Member on progress made towards the PIP Goals.  Leader should identify if the Team Member is On Track or Off Track in the appropriate field.</field-label>

    <field-description>Check-In Recap</field-description>

    <table-row-label lang="en_US">Check-In Recap</table-row-label>

    <table-column id="start" type="date" required="false" validate-start-before-due="true" cascade-update="push-down">

      <column-label>Start Date</column-label>

    </table-column>

    <table-column id="desc" type="textarea" required="true" cascade-update="push-down">

      <column-label>On Track or Off Track?</column-label>

    </table-column>

    <table-column id="actual" type="textarea" required="false" cascade-update="push-down">

      <column-label>Progress Update</column-label>

    </table-column>

  </field-definition>

Goal Plan plan layout:

Goal plan XML for form layout:

<form-layout><![CDATA[## Consider displaying goal category (if applicable) if mapping goals across all categories in one objective section

 

<div align="left">

  <b>${field.category}</b>

</div>#set ($group = "name-state")

 

<table width="100%" cellspacing="0" cellpadding="0">

  <tr>

    <td valign="top" width="10%">#if ($display.desc) <b>${label.desc}:</b><br>

     ${field.desc} #end</td>

 

    <td> </td>

 

    <td valign="top" width="20">#if ($display.metric-lookup-table) <b>${label.metric-lookup-table}:</b> ${field.metric-lookup-table} #end</td>

 

    <td> </td>

  </tr>

</table>]]></form-layout>

On PM form, Other Details tab is blank

Meta section of PM template has other details tab enabled:

<tab-permission type="enabled">
      <role-name>*</role-name>
      <tab refid="other-details"/>
      <route-step stepid="*"/>
   </tab-permission>

 

What am I missing that prevents the milestones field to display on the PM form?

Thank you.

entry.title: Adapt UI Different Approach between P and Q

So my questions are:

  1. How can I have app adaptions like in P, in Q and how do I assign business roles to it?

  2. How can these adaptations then being transported to P?

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.content:

1.

Enabling Key User Adaptation

2.

Publishing UI Changes

From <https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/4aa356f89e5a4409a9efe4dda4ddcb7e.html?version=202210.latest>

From <https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/92e77fa22fcd44e18e535c5cde68573b.html?version=202210.latest> 

 

3.

Deleting UI Changes from the Productive System

From <https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/4aa356f89e5a4409a9efe4dda4ddcb7e.html?version=202210.latest>

entry.title: The correct order to change SDCCN source flag destinations to point to the new solution Manager
entry.content:
We have reinstalled a new solution manager system, now with the SID of PSM. Our previous on-prem solution manager is now disabled. Managed system setup has been done for our S4 systems, using the new AWS solman, PSM. I am now trying to change managed systems' SDCCN source flag to the new solution Manager. I can not determine the proper way to do that. 

Managed system setup has been done for our S4 systems.  I am now trying to change SDCCN source flag destinations to point to the new solution Manager.   I can not determine the proper way to do that.  I reviewed these:

but they were not specific to my issue.

In our SD1 S4 system, SDCCN – Goto settings --> Task specific, the old PSO solution manager system is still listed as the “source”.  I would like to follow the proper sequence to remove the old PSO solution manager, to the new PSM solution manager in sdccn of all of our managed systems. 

 

Please advise.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: The correct order to change SDCCN source flag destina...
entry.content:
Hi Customer,

Greetings from SAP Product Support.

I would like to recommend the following order to set the source flag to your new solman in SD1 SDCCN:

1. Delete the old BACK RFC pointing to the old PSO solution manager system that listed as the “source” in the managed system's SDCCN.
2. Delete the old BACK RFC that points to the old PSO system in the managed system in SM59.
3. Ensure the Managed System Configuration setup steps are finished without any error in your new solman.
4. Add the New BACK RFC destination manually in SDCCN in your managed system and set the "Source" flag via SDCCN->Goto -> Settings -> Task specific -> RFC destinations -> Settings -> Destinations)

Hope the above helps to clarify.

Sincerely,
Yuki
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Story/report to show all members of a Public Dimension

entry.title: Re: Integration Suite is not available for subscription i...

Hi, I am in the same situation that I want to use Integration suite but cannot subscribe that.

entry.title: How to execute SI-CHECK when there are multiple production clients?
entry.content:
We are planning to migrate our ECC system with multiple production clients to an S/4HANA system with a brownfield conversion.
I have reviewed materials such as SAP Note 2270339 , but I couldn't find specific guidance on executing the SI-CHECK process for multiple production clients.
Regarding the SI-CHECK process, I would like to clarify the following:
・What steps are necessary for each client?
・Are there any steps that can be omitted for specific clients?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to execute SI-CHECK when there are multiple produ...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

According to Note 2399707 question "6.6. In which client should I perform the simplification item checks?" and SAP S/4HANA Simplification Item Check - How to do ... - SAP Community, it needs to execute the Relevance Check on each of the productive clients, then execute the Relevance Check & Consistency Check on the client 000 as it is the same client that SUM will run during the technical conversion. Only running the checks in client 000 will ensure, that data in all clients is checked for inconsistencies. If you run the check in any other client, some of the checks will only return check results for this specific client. Please refer to the following steps:

On all productive clients:

  1. Start report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK in transaction SA38.
  2. In the Simplification Item Check Options section, choose the target SAP S/4HANA product version.
  3. Choose New relevance check in Online mode
  4. Execute the check to receive the simplification item list.

On client 000:

  1. Start report /SDF/RC_START_CHECK in transaction SA38.
  2. In the Simplification Item Check Options section, choose the target SAP S/4HANA product version.
  3. Choose New relevance & consistency check as background job 
  4. Execute the check to receive the simplification item list.
  5. Review the results and resolve all inconsistency. 

Best regards,
Vera Jiang

entry.title: Re: 请问怎样修改S-user账号的密码？
entry.content:
Dear customer,

To reset S-user individual password, kindly refer to KBA 1808560 and reset the password via the URL https://accounts.sap.com/ui/createForgottenPasswordMail?spId=55365985e4b07dc3abdfc16c&targetUrl=&sourceUrl=.

Thanks and best regards,
Joanna Zhao
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Unable to send the IDOC from SAP to PI system
entry.content:
Thanks Avinash for posting this detailed response. I embraced point 3 as I was trying to re use RFC Destination and the ProgramID for IDoc_AAE which was prviously created for RFC Adapter Sender channel and ended with same issue.

Those who encounter this very issue, take note that IDoc Sender Communication Channel will not receive anything and will not even show error on Ping Test. However, when you get into RFC Sender Channel, errors will be reflected and it will be similar to what you will see in SM58.

Is it necessary to perform CVI_COCKPIT for each client?

entry.title: Re: AGGREGATION ON CDS VIEW and Filtering data with where clause issue
entry.content:
Thanks for reply.

Tried already.

If CDS views run independently the result is as follows.

CDS1 has 4 entries having different werks and lgort. It is as expected.

CDS2 make it one entry by summing  LABST for CDS1 4 entries. It looks fine.

 program also gives one entry which is fine but it is same as CDS2 ( which has SUM of all weeks, lgort).. is not expected because in where clause I passed hard-coded value to CDS1 fields same as it is in database. I don't think conversion issue here. It should have first filtered based on where clause of CDS1 and then SUM through CDS2.

entry.title: Re: Multiple bank in bank selection (FPY1 Payment run) depending the contract account
entry.content: Hi Amlan, thanks a lot, it's working :

entry.title: Re: 公司间销售业务的按单生产业务
entry.content: Hi Feng_chen，感谢你的回复。我看到样例是使用1HO，但是，在跨国贸易中这个功能不适用。在跨国贸易中，集团公司是贸易公司A和制造公司B。而贸易公司A与外部客户的确认时间点是以客户签收单，而贸易公司A与制造公司B是以FOB离港日作为确认成本和收入，这两个日期是不一致，且时间间隔会跨月。而1HO中，有两个问题：1）必须先开了对外部客户的客户发票后，才能开集团内部发票，这是时间上是有跨月差异；2）销售发货时，贸易公司A与制造公司B同时确认了成本，这也是在时间上存在差异。所以，面对这种跨国贸易的情况，我才想使用5D2来应对，但是，突然发现，制造公司B不支持Make-to-order 或WBS。

entry.title: Re: SAP Build process automation - Document Template - Error
entry.content:
Hi,

We created the subaccount again and the probelm was resolved

entry.title: Remediation step on weak TLS vulnerability
entry.content:
I need on how to remediate system vulnerability reported by our VA tool. It is related to TLS. The step suggested by the tool is very general and not specific for SAP ECC on Suse Linux environment. Please find the attached file for detailed vulnerability.

 

Regards,

 

Zaharah


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Remediation step on weak TLS vulnerability
entry.content:
I can provide a general approach to addressing TLS vulnerabilities in such an environment. Here are the steps you can follow:

  1. Review the Vulnerability Report: Carefully read the vulnerability report generated by your VA tool. Identify the specific TLS-related issues, such as weak ciphers, outdated protocols, or misconfigurations.

  2. Update OpenSSL: Ensure that OpenSSL is up-to-date on your SUSE Linux system. You can update OpenSSL using the following commands:

    sudo zypper refresh
sudo zypper update openssl

  3. Configure Strong Ciphers and Protocols:

    • Edit the OpenSSL configuration file, typically located at /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf or /etc/ssl/openssl.conf.
    • Ensure that only strong ciphers and protocols are enabled. For example, you can disable SSLv2 and SSLv3 and enable TLSv1.2 and TLSv1.3:
      [system_default_sect]
MinProtocol = TLSv1.2
CipherString = DEFAULT@SECLEVEL=2

  4. Update SAP Profile Parameters:

    • Log in to your SAP system and navigate to the profile parameters.
    • Update the parameters related to TLS/SSL to enforce strong ciphers and protocols. For example:
      ssl/ciphersuites = 135:PFS:HIGH::EC_P256:EC_HIGH
ssl/client_ciphersuites = 150:PFS:HIGH::EC_P256:EC_HIGH
ssl/protocols = !SSLv2:!SSLv3:TLSv1.2:TLSv1.3

  5. Restart SAP Services: After making the necessary changes, restart the SAP services to apply the new configurations. You can do this using the following commands:

    sudo su - <sidadm>
stopsap
startsap

  6. Verify the Configuration: Use tools like openssl s_client or online SSL/TLS testing services to verify that the changes have been applied correctly and that the system is no longer vulnerable.

entry.title: SAP client connect server failed.(security certificate)

entry.title: Re: OData Provisioning: A New Capability of SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Assistance Required for Enhancing ME5A Report
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I hope this message finds you well.

We are currently facing a requirement related to the ME5A report that needs your expert guidance. The details are as follows:

Requirement Overview:

  • We need the ME5A report, which typically shows purchase requisitions, to be updated such that if a purchase requisition exists for a material, the report should also display the header material (finished product).
  • The purchase requisitions should be shown as sales order-specific and collective.
  • In the MD04 transaction, when we open the order report, purchase requisitions are displayed, and at a higher level, subassemblies are shown with their corresponding finished products.
  • We need these finished product numbers to be included in the ME5A report.

Request for Guidance:

Please suggest how we can achieve this enhancement. Specifically:

  • Are there any relevant function modules (FMs) or BAPIs that can be used for this purpose?
  • Can you advise on the linkage between the necessary tables, such as EBAN (Purchase Requisition), MARA (Material Master), VBAK (Sales Order Header), and STPO (BOM Items)?

Your expertise on how to approach this task or any pointers to documentation would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you for your support and assistance

entry.title: Re: PSS not working after CUA implementation
entry.content:
Hello,

What final solution found on this issue. I am facing same issue, for GRC PSS link login error is getting as "User xxxxx does not exist in SAP system"

Muzammil

entry.title: Re: I am trying to get the value of data table using getv...
entry.content: Hi Bill_Froelich, I want to get the inputted value in a data table. Let's say my data table has two columns: Material Name: This column contains material names coming from a REST API. Quantity: This column is where I will enter the values of the quantity for each material. After clicking on the submit button, all the materials and their quantities should be posted through a POST API. This is my use case. I am following the tutorial about data tables in this blog: What's New in Mobile Development Kit Client 23.4 url of blog - https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/what-s-new-in-mobile-development-kit-client-23-4/ba-p/13557735

entry.title: Re: How to consider the stock from Blocked/Quality in MRP...
entry.content:
Hi,

The Quality stock will be considered in the MRP as per the standard SAP. 

If you want to exclude the quality stock for a few meterials then it can be handdled by two ways.

The BAdI mentioned by an expert @DominikTylczyn. Other ways would be a sepearte storage location and exclude it from the MRP. In OMIR for the storage location to be set to 1 if you are in ECC and assigned to those materials . If you are using S4H then MRP area would be needed.

entry.title: SAP Security Patch Day – December 2024
entry.content:
On December 10, 2024, SAP released its monthly Security Patch Day updates, addressing several vulnerabilities across various products, including SAP NetWeaver AS, SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP Web Dispatcher, and more. Below, we provide an overview of the most critical vulnerabilities, their risk levels, and recommended actions to safeguard your SAP landscape.

Critical and High-Priority Vulnerabilities

  1. [CVE-2024-47578] Multiple Vulnerabilities in SAP NetWeaver AS for JAVA (Adobe Document Services)

    • CVSS Score: 9.1 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:H/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS for JAVA (Adobe Document Services)
    • Description: A set of vulnerabilities that can compromise sensitive information and allow significant impact on system integrity and availability.
    • Priority: HotNews

    This vulnerability requires immediate attention as attackers with administrative privileges (PR:H) can exploit it to access critical data and disrupt SAP systems.

  2. [CVE-2024-47590] Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) in SAP Web Dispatcher

    • CVSS Score: 8.8 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:R/S:U/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP Web Dispatcher
    • Description: A high-severity XSS vulnerability that enables attackers to inject malicious code into the web interface, potentially leading to session hijacking, content spoofing, and more.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

    This vulnerability is exploitable without prior authentication (PR:N), emphasizing the urgency of applying the patch.

  3. [CVE-2024-47586] NULL Pointer Dereference in SAP NetWeaver Application Server for ABAP and ABAP Platform

    • CVSS Score: 7.5 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP
    • Description: This vulnerability can lead to a denial of service (DoS), causing critical unavailability of the affected system.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

  4. [CVE-2024-54197] Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in SAP NetWeaver Administrator (System Overview)

    • CVSS Score: 7.2 (AV:N/AC:L/PR:N/UI:N/S:C/C:L/I:L/A:N)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver Administrator
    • Description: SSRF vulnerabilities can allow unauthenticated attackers to access internal resources, potentially exposing sensitive data or leveraging trusted connections.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

  5. [CVE-2024-54198] Information Disclosure via RFC in SAP NetWeaver Application Server ABAP

    • CVSS Score: 8.5 (AV:N/AC:H/PR:L/UI:N/S:C/C:H/I:H/A:H)
    • Affected Component: SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP
    • Description: This high-risk vulnerability enables attackers with low privileges (PR:L) to exploit complex scenarios (AC:H) to access critical information or compromise system integrity.
    • Priority: Correction with high priority

Medium- and Low-Priority Vulnerabilities

  1. [CVE-2024-47582] XML Entity Expansion in SAP NetWeaver AS JAVA

    • CVSS Score: 5.3
    • Description: A vulnerability in XML processing that could lead to system overload or potential denial of service.

  2. [CVE-2024-32732] Information Disclosure in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform

    • CVSS Score: 5.3
    • Description: An issue that could result in the leakage of non-critical but sensitive information.

  3. [CVE-2024-42375] Multiple Unrestricted File Upload Vulnerabilities in SAP BusinessObjects Business Intelligence Platform

    • CVSS Score: 4.3
    • Description: Allows attackers with limited privileges to upload unwanted files, potentially affecting data integrity.

  4. [CVE-2024-47585] Missing Authorization Check in SAP NetWeaver AS ABAP and ABAP Platform

    • CVSS Score: 4.3
    • Description: Insufficient authorization checks could allow unauthorized access to specific data.

  5. [CVE-2024-47577] Information Disclosure in SAP Commerce Cloud

    • CVSS Score: 2.7
    • Description: A low-severity information disclosure vulnerability requiring administrator privileges.

  6. [CVE-2024-47576] DLL Hijacking in SAP Product Lifecycle Costing

    • CVSS Score: 3.3
    • Description: A local vulnerability requiring user privileges. It is relevant for securing developer and administrator workstations.

Conclusion

The December SAP Security Patch Day highlights critical fixes addressing a wide range of vulnerabilities. Many of these require immediate action to prevent potential data breaches, system compromises, or denial-of-service attacks.

We strongly encourage analyzing these vulnerabilities promptly and applying the necessary patches. Taking a proactive approach will minimize risks and maintain the security of your SAP landscapes.

 

SAP Penetration Testing: https://redrays.io/sap-penetration-testing/

SAP Security Training: https://www.udemy.com/course/sap-security-core-concepts-and-security-administration/
